headline: Agentic AI Gone Mainstream: Companies Shift From Pilots to Production, Agents Beat Chatbots, Microsoft Bundles AI to Save Seat-Based Revenue

stories.title: Agentic AI Is the Real Inflection Point—Not Bubbles
stories.summary: Stratechery's Ben Thompson argues we're not in an AI bubble at all; agents have moved from theory to actual functional systems reshaping how companies operate. Microsoft's gambit shows agents threaten traditional software business models.
stories.takeaway: Agents aren't copilots—they replace human labor and you can't unsee that once you've seen it work.
stories.strategyTake: Companies moving from managing people to orchestrating human-agent teams; old playbooks don't work anymore.

stories.title: McKinsey: Only 1/3 of Companies Actually Scaling AI—High Performers Redesign Workflows, Not Just Buy Models
stories.summary: 88% of orgs use AI somewhere, but just one-third are scaling it. The gap? Winners redesign how work happens (organization first, tech second), while others stay stuck in pilot purgatory.
stories.takeaway: AI success is behavioral, not technical—org design and top-down commitment beat model quality.
stories.strategyTake: Top performers commit 20%+ of digital budgets to AI and couple it with aggressive workforce restructuring, not isolated pilots.

stories.title: Oracle + Nvidia's GTC Moment: Inference Economics Just Flipped—The Real AI Race Is Compute Optimization, Not Model Training
stories.summary: Nvidia announced 35x inference speedups and a $1T+ demand forecast (double from 6 months ago) for production agents, not training runs. Oracle slashing 20–30K jobs to fund AI infrastructure signals the entire industry is repricing what matters.
stories.takeaway: The era of 'let's train a model' is over—winners are optimizing inference workloads for 24/7 agent swarms.
stories.strategyTake: Enterprise deployment costs are about running agents constantly, not one-time training; margin pressure on SaaS is structural.

stories.title: OpenAI Killed Sora, Microsoft Raised E7 Prices 2x: Why Compute Allocation Is the New Kingmaker
stories.summary: OpenAI quietly shut down Sora public API (too expensive per-minute), while Microsoft bundled AI into a $99/month E7 tier (vs. $20/month E5) to offset seat-based erosion. Both moves show the winners are those who control the narrative around what AI is worth.
stories.takeaway: Flashy consumer AI features disappear fast when they compete for GPU; business model innovation beats feature innovation rn.
stories.strategyTake: Every product line now fights for compute first, users second; margin math is breaking legacy SaaS pricing.

watchNext.title: BCG Henderson Institute: AI Will Reshape More Jobs Than It Replaces—But Skills Gap Is Brutal
watchNext.why: Only 5% of jobs are 'amplified' by AI; the rest reshape entirely. Software engineers + lawyers in advisory are the only safe zones. Orgs that don't reskill fast lose competitiveness, fast.

watchNext.title: Harvard Business Review: AI Doesn't Reduce Work—It Intensifies It (aka 'productivity paradox')
watchNext.why: Berkeley research shows AI adoption actually *increases* workload thru verification, validation, and facilitation overhead. Knowing this changes how you staff and structure teams.
